Title: Overexpression of a Gene Encoding Trigonelline Synthase from Areca catechu L. Promotes Drought Resilience in Transgenic Arabidopsis

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: AcTS encodes a 373 amino-acid protein from a 1122 bp CDS and contains three conserved SAM-binding motifs shared with Coffea arabica trigonelline synthases (CaTS1 and CaTS2); phylogenetically, AcTS clusters with CaTS1 and CaTS2, distinct from Arabidopsis, soybean and rice trigonelline synthases.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. AcTS was identified from the Areca catechu genome as a trigonelline synthase gene (AC10G024220), encoding a 373-aa N-methyltransferase with conserved SAM-binding motifs and clustering phylogenetically with Coffea trigonelline synthases.
2. Drought strongly induces AcTS expression in areca seedlings, with ~80-fold and ~6-fold transcript increases in leaves and roots at 2 days after treatment, correlating with marked accumulation of trigonelline in leaves but not roots.
3. Transgenic Arabidopsis lines overexpressing AcTS (OxAcTS14 and OxAcTS17) accumulate higher endogenous trigonelline and exhibit enhanced drought tolerance, showing only slight wilting after prolonged drought and rapid recovery after rehydration compared with wild-type Col-0.
4. AcTS overexpression and exogenous trigonelline both promote Arabidopsis root growth under mannitol-induced osmotic stress, supporting a role for AcTS-mediated trigonelline production in improving drought and osmotic stress resilience.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AcTS encodes a trigonelline synthase whose overexpression elevates trigonelline accumulation and markedly improves drought tolerance and root growth under osmotic stress in transgenic Arabidopsis; in areca seedlings AcTS is strongly drought-inducible and associated with increased trigonelline levels in leaves.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a trigonelline synthase (N-methyltransferase) catalyzing the methylation of nicotinic acid using S-adenosyl-L-methionine to produce trigonelline, thereby increasing cellular levels of this compatible osmolyte under drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Participates in drought stress response and osmotic adjustment through trigonelline-mediated protection, contributing to improved drought resilience and root growth under osmotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Trigonelline biosynthesis: nicotinic acid N-methylation to trigonelline catalyzed by trigonelline synthase (N-methyltransferase).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: N-methyltransferase (trigonelline synthase) containing conserved S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM) binding motifs.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under drought, AcTS transcript levels are strongly induced in areca leaves and roots: at 2 days after treatment, expression increases to about 80-fold in leaves and 6-fold in roots relative to controls; leaf expression remains significantly higher than controls at 4–8 days before dropping to control levels by day 10, while root expression is elevated at 4–6 days and significantly downregulated in the treatment group from day 8.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: AC10G024220 (Areca catechu trigonelline synthase gene; genome assembly id JAHSVC000000000; BioSample SAMN19591864).
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Areca catechu genome assembly JAHSVC000000000 (locus AC10G024220).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed by withholding water from areca seedlings for 10 consecutive days; osmotic stress induced by 150 mM mannitol in Arabidopsis growth medium; exogenous application of 1 mg/L trigonelline; drought treatment of soil-grown Arabidopsis for 14–16 days followed by rehydration.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought tolerance and abiotic stress response in Areca catechu via trigonelline biosynthesis and function of the trigonelline synthase gene AcTS.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress: drought tolerance and osmotic stress response (root growth under mannitol).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In areca leaves, trigonelline content under drought increases from 695.35 μg/g to 1125.21 μg/g after 10 days of water shortage, while in roots no significant trigonelline change is detected; proline levels in leaves reach 804.98 μg/g DW (5.88-fold higher than controls) and in roots 45.35 μg/g DW (3.47-fold higher) after 10 days drought; AcTS transcript abundance in areca leaves and roots at 2 days drought increases by ~80-fold and ~6-fold, respectively, compared to controls; in transgenic Arabidopsis, trigonelline content in OxAcTS14 and OxAcTS17 is approximately 2-fold and 1.2-fold higher than in Col-0.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: In Arabidopsis, AcTS overexpression or exogenous trigonelline leads to longer primary roots and better-developed lateral roots under mannitol-induced osmotic stress, while root length is similar to wild type on standard half-strength MS medium.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Areca catechu seedlings; Arabidopsis thaliana wild-type Col-0; transgenic Arabidopsis lines overexpressing AcTS (OxAcTS14 and OxAcTS17); growth media including half-strength MS, half-strength MS + 150 mM mannitol, half-strength MS + 1 mg/L trigonelline, and half-strength MS + mannitol + trigonelline; peat soil/vermiculite (3:1) pots.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Bioinformatic search of the Areca catechu genome to identify N-methyltransferase candidates; cloning of full-length AcTS CDS; phylogenetic and conserved motif analysis; qRT-PCR to quantify AcTS transcript abundance in leaves and roots under drought; HPLC-based trigonelline quantification; colorimetric proline assay; chlorophyll fluorescence measurements (Fv/Fm, Y(II), qP, NPQ); generation of AcTS-overexpressing Arabidopsis via Agrobacterium-mediated floral dip; drought and mannitol treatments; measurement of primary root length and phenotypic analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional identification by transgenic overexpression of AcTS in Arabidopsis Col-0 (OxAcTS14 and OxAcTS17), followed by trigonelline quantification and drought/mannitol stress assays; expression analysis of AcTS in areca leaves and roots under drought by qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: AcTS was identified by bioinformatic search of the Areca catechu genome for genes annotated as N-methyltransferases (AC10G024220; JAHSVC000000000), followed by cloning of the full-length CDS from areca and functional screening of candidate N-methyltransferase genes via overexpression in Arabidopsis, where only AcTS increased endogenous trigonelline content.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: AcTS is a drought-inducible trigonelline synthase whose overexpression increases trigonelline levels and drought tolerance, providing a candidate gene to enhance drought resilience of areca seedlings and potentially other crops via manipulation of trigonelline-mediated osmotic adjustment.
